TextENGNew: ``Yeah. Sorry.@k@vS20/03/440300046. I suddenly got called in for a shift at my uncle’s shop today.``
``What? So club’s cancelled today then?``
``Yeah, ah-ha-ha, my apologies...!``
Mion pressed her palms together apologetically and flashed a grin.
``If you really want to do club that badly, you’re welcome to go ahead without this old man. The club locker is stuffed with games.@k@vS20/03/440300049. Just pick whichever one you like.``
The "club locker" was the fourth-dimensional storage space from which Mion would produce games for our club activities.
How she managed to cram so many games into that locker—which was even smaller than the janitor’s closet—remained a mystery...
And that wasn’t even counting the mountain of suspicious items (costumes?) stockpiled for punishment games...!
If we laid everything out on the floor to sort it, it would surely fill the entire classroom.
``...I’ll pass. I have a feeling that the moment I touch it, everything inside would come avalanching out.``
``Ah-ha-ha-ha! Hmm, yeah, you’re probably right.@k@vS20/03/440300051. Amateurs probably shouldn’t meddle with it. Heh heh heh!``
Mion laughed heartily, as if to say that mastering the locker was precisely what made her the club president.
``...Mii. The teacher is looking for the key to the gym storage room.@k@vS20/05/440500011. You wouldn’t happen to know where it is, would you?``
``Huh? ...Didn’t I return it to the teachers’ lounge?``
Mion said this while rummaging through her pockets.
...Judging by her expression, the key she thought she’d returned was apparently still right there in her pocket.
``See, I knew it was Mion-san~! I was absolutely, positively, one hundred percent certain it had to be you!``
``...I was right too. Satoko and I were both right.``
Satoko and Rika-chan clapped their hands together gleefully, celebrating their victory.
``Oh, shut it!``
Bonk! Thwack!
Mion drove her elbows into Satoko’s and Rika-chan’s heads, then marched off toward the teachers’ lounge.
``Oh, right. Have you two heard?@k@vS20/01/440100048. Mion’s got work today, so club’s cancelled.``
``Oh, is that so?! How terribly dull.``
changeReason: Original uses 'droll' (meaning amusing/funny) but JPN つまらない means 'boring/dull'; new translation correctly conveys disappointment.
New translation renders Rika's utterance as 'Mii' (direct from JPN みぃ), rather than the original's creative localization 'Meep,' changing a recognizable character catchphrase.

TextENGNew: Both of them looked disappointed.
...Say what you will, but Mion’s club was the thing we all looked forward to most about coming to school.
Once you learned it was cancelled, it was only natural to feel deflated.
``So Mion has a part-time job, huh. ...What kind of work does she actually do?``
Whenever Mion cancelled club, it was almost always because of her job.
But she didn’t seem to go there every day.
Sometimes she’d go two or three days in a row, and other times she wouldn’t go at all.
...Was there really such an irregular part-time job?
``...She doesn’t work at any particular shop on a regular basis.``
``It’s not a set workplace? ...So she’s like a day laborer?``
Mion in split-toed work boots and a hard hat, dripping with sweat at a construction site...@k...I couldn’t picture it.
changeReason: Original says 'socks' and 'factory' but JPN 足袋 means 'split-toed work boots' (tabi) and 工事現場 means 'construction site,' not a factory. New translation corrects both.

TextENGNew: ``Mion-san fills in at her uncles’ shops whenever they’re short-handed. She’s like a jack-of-all-trades, apparently.``
``Ah... I see. Come to think of it, she did mention working at her uncle’s place before.@k@vS20/01/440100052. I think I’ve heard that before.``
``...She has many uncles who own shops, so Mii is in very high demand.``
Many uncles who own shops?@k That many?
``Oh, you didn’t know, Keiichi-san? There are quite a few shops in Okinomiya run by Mion-san’s relatives.``
``Huh... I had no idea.@k@vS20/01/440100054. ...There are that many different kinds?``
``Yes. There’s a bakery, a greengrocer, a ramen shop,@k@vS20/04/440400023. and plenty more besides.@k@vS20/04/440400024. That toy store where we held club the other day is one of them too.``
I was speechless.@k...That’s... incredible!
``...Does Mion’s clan just have some kind of innate business sense? Running that many different businesses is pretty amazing!``
``...There are others too—finance, real estate, apparel, amusement. They’re into all sorts of things, apparently.``
Some oddly suspicious ones popped up in there, but anyway!@k I got the point—they had their fingers in a lot of pies.
``So the reason she’s so strict about return dates whenever you borrow something is because the blood of financiers runs in her veins! That explains everything!``
``...Once I completely forgot to return the Billionaire board game I borrowed from Mii, and she threatened to make me play a real-life version of it.``
...Rika-chan playing a real-life Billionaire game...
...Something tells me she’d shrewdly claw her way to the top as an IT mogul...
``Playing a real-life Billionaire game sounds rather thrilling~!``
...As for Satoko...
...She’d probably pile up grudges from everyone around her and meet some spectacular downfall.
Well, that would be fitting for Satoko.
Rika-chan and I patted Satoko on the head.
``...?! Wh-Why are you petting me?! ...?!``
``...Because you’re such a good girl, Satoko. Pat, pat.``
Satoko was flustered and confused, but she seemed to grasp that she was being teased.
